title: Zero-Shot Multilingual Translation
concept: Pretraining a single Transformer on many language pairs lets it discover a shared representation space, allowing translation between languages it never saw paired in training data.

exercises.prompt: What is a multilingual AI model?

options.text: A model that only understands a single language
A model trained on many different languages simultaneously
A model that translates code into human speech

exercises.explain: A multilingual model is trained on multiple languages at the same time, allowing it to learn general translation patterns.

cards.title: The Ultimate Polyglot
cards.body: Imagine learning to translate languages you were never taught to translate together! Traditional AI needed direct examples for every language pair. Today, we'll discover how a single AI model can translate between languages it has never seen paired up before.

cards.title: Zero-Shot Translation
cards.body: Zero-Shot Translation is the ability of a single AI model to translate between a language pair (like Italian to Japanese) that it never saw paired together during its training. It does this by using a shared understanding it built from learning other language combinations.

cards.title: The Multilingual Bridge
cards.body: Imagine a translator who knows how to translate Spanish to English, and English to French. They have never translated Spanish directly to French before. However, because they translate both languages into a shared 'meaning' in their head (English acts as the bridge), they can translate Spanish directly to French on their very first try!

cards.title: Translation without Training Pairs
cards.body: Suppose we train a Transformer model on French-to-English and English-to-Spanish translation data. We never show it a single French-to-Spanish example. During testing, we give it a French sentence and ask it to output Spanish. The model successfully translates it! This is zero-shot translation in action.

cards.title: Do We Need Every Pair?
cards.body: You might think a model needs to practice translating every specific language pair to do it well. In reality, a single large model trained on many languages learns a universal 'concept space' that automatically bridges new language pairs.

cards.title: Mapping the Languages
cards.body: Instead of building separate pathways for every single language pair, a multilingual Transformer maps all languages into a unified center of meaning.

left.text: Requires training separate models or pathways for every pair (e.g., German-French, German-Italian, French-Italian).

right.text: One model maps German, French, and Italian to a single shared space, unlocking translation between any of them.

options.text: Translating a language with zero errors on the first try
Translating between a language pair never seen together during training
Translating text without using any Transformer layers
Translating code from Python to Javascript

exercises.explain: Zero-shot translation refers to translating between a language pair that the model was never explicitly trained on.

exercises.prompt: A single Transformer model can translate between two languages even if it has never seen a single translation example between those two specific languages.
exercises.explain: True! A single multilingual Transformer can translate between language pairs it has never seen together by utilizing its shared internal representations.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how multilingual models translate unseen pairs.
exercises.sentence: Multilingual models translate unseen language pairs by mapping them to a _ representation space.
exercises.explain: By learning many languages, the model maps different words to the same shared concept space, enabling zero-shot translations.

exercises.prompt: Which of these scenarios represents a Zero-Shot Multilingual Translation?

options.text: An AI is trained on Spanish-to-English and translates Spanish to English.
An AI is trained on French-to-English and translates French to English.
An AI trained on Spanish-to-English and English-to-French translates Spanish directly to French.

exercises.explain: The third option represents a zero-shot translation because the model has only seen translations involving English, yet it is asked to translate directly between French and Spanish without prior training on that specific pair.

exercises.prompt: How does a single multilingual Transformer perform zero-shot translation between two languages it never saw paired during training?

options.text: By training a separate small model for every possible pair of languages on earth
By mapping all languages into a shared meaning space, allowing it to bridge unseen pairs
By translating everything to English first and displaying the English text to the user
By copying and pasting words from an online dictionary during the translation process

exercises.explain: Zero-shot translation happens because the model converts all source languages into a shared neural representation space (an 'interlingua') before translating them into any target language, even for language pairs it never practiced together.
